symptoms: Elongated, reddish-brown pustules on stems and leaves. Stems become weak and lodge easily.
treatment_text: Apply chemical fungicide like Propiconazole 25% EC at 2 ml/liter or Tebuconazole 250 EC at 1.5 ml/liter.
severity_scale: Orange powder rubs off on fingers. High severity means stem coverage > 50%.

symptoms: Water-soaked stripes on leaf blades, turning yellow-to-white from the tips downwards.
treatment_text: Spray Streptocycline 15g + Copper Oxychloride 500g in 200 liters of water per acre.
severity_scale: Wavy gray-brown stripe lesions running down leaf edges.

symptoms: Upward curling of leaf margins, thickening of leaf veins, and visual stunted growth.
treatment_text: Target the whitefly vector. Spray Imidacloprid 17.8% SL at 0.5 ml/liter or Thiamethoxam 25 WG at 0.3g/liter.
severity_scale: Cup-like leaf structures or visual leaf crumpling on young shoots.

symptoms: Third or fourth leaves wither. Splitting the cane longitudinally reveals red pith with white spots and a sour alcoholic odor.
treatment_text: Uproot affected stools and spray Carbendazim 50% WP at 2g/liter on surrounding soils.
severity_scale: Inner stalk tissue is dull red with horizontal white spots.

symptoms: Small brown/black spots on older leaves expanding with concentric rings (target board pattern).
treatment_text: Spray Mancozeb 75% WP at 2.5g/liter or Chlorothalonil 75% WP at 2g/liter.
severity_scale: Concentric dark brown target spots starting on lower leaves.
